sources.scope: 2020-2023 generation Creta India range: 1.5 l MPi petrol (1497 cc, 6MT/IVT), 1.4 l Kappa turbo GDi petrol (1353 cc, 103 kW / 242 Nm, 7-speed DCT), 50-litre fuel tank; trim plan E/EX/S/SX/SX(O)/Knight with the turbo in upper trims (p3-p4).
sources.supportingText: Specifications and trim plan only; the brochure makes no ethanol or E20 statement. Archived OEM document; local copy retained at DataResearch/brochures/hyundai-creta/.
sources.title: Hyundai CRETA brochure, Jan-Feb 2023 (Wayback Machine snapshot, 26 March 2023)

sources.scope: Fuel requirements for petrol-engine Creta manufactured September 2020 - January 2024: 'Do not use gasohol containing more than 20% ethanol, and do not use petrol or gasohol containing any methanol.' (p5).
sources.supportingText: A usage boundary, not an E20 certification or warranty statement; the manual also advises discontinuing gasohol if drivability problems occur. Local copy retained at DataResearch/brochures/hyundai-creta/.
sources.title: Creta owner's manual, September 2020 - January 2024 generation (Wayback Machine snapshot, 19 April 2025)

sources.scope: Owner-reported real-world fuel efficiency for the 2nd-gen Creta; 1.4 turbo DCT owners report roughly 11-13 km/l in city running and 15-16 km/l on highway; ARAI figure 16.8 km/l for the 1.4 turbo DCT.
sources.supportingText: SECONDARY source (enthusiast publication, owner reports) — used for indicative tuning seeds and the ARAI figure under the two-tier policy; not an OEM document.

sources.scope: Official Maruti Suzuki launch announcement for Alto K10 current generation (August 2022). Confirms: 1.0L K-Series Dual Jet Dual VVT engine (998cc implied), manual transmission (5-speed), AGS transmission (5-speed), ARAI mileage 24.39 km/l (manual) and 24.90 km/l (AGS), power 49kW (66.62 PS), torque 89 Nm. Hatchback body style. HEARTECT platform.
sources.supportingText: Press release does not specify: engine code designation (K10C vs K-Series numeric), fuel tank capacity, complete trim variant names, trim-specific transmission applicability, E20 compatibility status (certification occurred post-April 2023 per separate corporate announcement).
sources.title: Maruti Suzuki All-New Alto K10 Launch Press Release

sources.scope: Current OEM product specification page. Confirms: 998cc displacement, K-Series Dual Jet Dual VVT engine, max power 50.4 kW (65.7 bhp) @ 5600 rpm, max torque 89 Nm, fuel tank 27L, ARAI mileage 24.39 km/l (manual) and 24.90 km/l (AGS), transmission options: 5-speed manual and AGS, trim variants (STD, LXi, VXi, VXi+, LXi CNG), hatchback body type, dimensions 3530x1490x1520mm.
sources.supportingText: Engine code (K10C) not explicitly stated; referred to as 'K-Series Dual Jet Dual VVT'. Trim-specific transmission applicability not detailed. E20 compatibility not mentioned on product page (requires separate certification documentation).
sources.title: Maruti Suzuki Alto K10 Official Product Page

sources.scope: CarDekho.com specifications page for Grand Vitara 1.5L petrol variants (MT and AT). Provides engine displacement (1462 cc), fuel tank capacity (45 Litres), body style (Compact SUV), transmission types (Manual and Automatic), and published mileage figures (KMPL) for each variant. Verbatim: Fuel Tank Capacity: 45 Litres; Engine Displacement: 1462 CC; Body Style: Compact SUV; Transmission options: Manual (MT), Automatic (AT); Petrol City Mileage: 21.11 kmpl (MT); Petrol Highway Mileage: 21.97 kmpl (AT)
sources.supportingText: SECONDARY SOURCE - not OEM primary document. CarDekho aggregates manufacturer specifications and customer testing data. Engine displacement, fuel tank capacity, body style, and transmission types confirmed from this source; exact OEM compliance and certification still requires archived OEM brochure PDF. Mileage figures cited here represent CarDekho's aggregation of claimed values (KMPL: 21.11 manual, 21.97 automatic); ARAI-certified lab values unavailable.
sources.title: Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara Specifications

sources.scope: Maruti Suzuki official press release announcing launch of Grand Vitara compact SUV on 12 September 2022 via NEXA channel. Intended to confirm 1.5L petrol engine, manual and automatic transmission options, and market positioning as mid-size SUV with strong hybrid alternative.
sources.supportingText: URL https://www.marutisuzuki.com/press-release returned HTTP 404 at research date (2026-07-17). Press release text cannot be retrieved. September 2022 launch timeframe derived from trade press consensus but not verified against OEM primary source.
sources.title: Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara Launch Announcement
